revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jūsanpōzuka Site]][[ja:十三宝塚遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jūsanpōzuka Site]][[ja:十三宝塚遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jūsanpōzuka Site]][[ja:十三宝塚遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jūsanpōzuka Site]][[ja:十三宝塚遺跡]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in Isesaki, Kantō, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Jūsanpōzuka iseki | native_name =十三宝塚遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Jūsanpōzuka iseki | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jūsanpōzuka iseki | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|48|N|139|14|13|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs ={{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara|1=Nara period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Jūsanpōzuka iseki'''|十三宝塚遺跡|}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} with the ruins of a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara|1=Nara period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} regional administrative complex, located in the Sakaiiyoku neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138137|title=十三宝塚遺跡|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== In the late {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, after the establishment of a centralized government under the ''[[Ritsuryō]]'' system and [[Taika Reform]]s, local rule over the {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=provinces|1=Provinces of Japan}} was standardized under a ''[[kokufu]]'' (provincial capital), and each province was divided into smaller {{ill|Districts of Japan|en|Districts of Japan|qid=Q1122846|lt=administrative districts|1=Districts of Japan}}, known as (郡, gun, kōri), composed of 2–20 townships in 715 AD.<ref name="CHJ">{{cite book |title=Cambridge History of Japan vol. II (p.31f.) |author=Shively, Donald H. |author2=McCullough, William H. |publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]] |year=1999}}</ref> Each of the units featured an administrative complex that was constructed using a plan that was loosely based on modern Chinese architecture.
The Jūsanpōzuka site is located on a plateau formed at the end of the foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}, east of the city. It consists of the ruins of an enclosure with dimensions of 180 meters east-to-west and 250 meters north-to-south, with the remnants of a moat on the north, west, and south sides. In the north-central part of this enclosure is the remains of a secondary enclosure with the foundations of a large gate, and the foundation of a large pillared building on a platform the center. This secondary enclosure was trapezoidal with 82 meters on the south and west sides, 92 meters on the east side, and 60 meter on the north side. Both enclosures were protected by palisades. Since this arrangement is typical for the layout of a county-level administrative complex as stipulated under the ''Ritsuryō'' system, it is presumed to have been the Nara period county seat for a "Saki County" in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} mentioned in ancient documents such as the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]''. This theory has been reinforced by the discovery of [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s with acronyms for many of the township names in the Saki area.
The site was backfilled after [[archaeological excavation]], and there is nothing but an empty field with an explanatory plaque. It is located approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Gōshi Station|en|Gōshi Station|qid=Q5627764|lt=Gōshi Station|1=Gōshi Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|qid=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/4283.html Isesaki City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
